rulings.Case Title: KESHA CROMARTIE VS. NORTHRIDGE COOPERATIVE HOMES,INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 8, 2024 line 1. RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. The petition for writ of mandate and all causes of action are sufficiently stated and are not uncertain. Declaration of Ravneet Sandhu is stricken, except for paragraphs 1, 2, 3, and 28. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SPALDING VS. VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 8, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions, legal authorities or general discussions) in support of each element of each cause of action. Plaintiff shall clearly label which cause of action is asserted against which defendant and include allegations pertinent to each specific cause of action as to each defendant against whom the cause of action is asserted. Complaint must be in clear and concise language. Plaintiff does not have leave to include any new causes of action. Plaintiff does not have leave to amend causes of action for strict liability and assault. If plaintiff wishes to assert these causes of action Plaintiff must file a motion for leave to amend. Plaintiff my use CACI as a guideline for elements of the causes of action. Late-filed oversized opposition is stricken. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of this order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HOMESPACE LLC VS. SHIRLEY HARTLING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 8, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK N.A.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege each element of each cause of action with requisite facts, conclusory allegations currently made in the operative complaint are insufficient. To avoid any future confusion regarding the proper department for this case, all future law and motion shall proceed in department 501 for as long as there is an active cause of action against defendant Hartling arising out of the lease. If there are no causes of action against Harling (e.g. not asserted in the amended complaint, dismissed, etc), law and motion shall proceed in department 302. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: GHNAIM PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHELLEY WIENKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S 2nd Amended Complaint For Non- Compliance With Court Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 8, 2024 line 10. DEFENDANT SHELLEY WIENKE'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR NON-COMPLIANCE WITH COURT ORDER is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any applicable authority for the purported motion to dismiss. To the extent, this motion was intended to serve as a pleading challenge under California Code of Civil Procedure, the motion is DENIED as the Second Amended Complaint was filed pursuant to the Court's order on the Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint. Defendant shall file an answer within 5 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
